issue.name: Weak key size: 256 bits
issue.issueBackground: Classifier rationale: module flagged as interesting
issue.remediationBackground: N/A — verify finding first.
issue.issueDetail:
Key size 256 < recommended 2048
Verification steps: 1. Reproduce the request that produced this finding. 2. Confirm the observation is consistent across retries. 3. Determine whether it is a real issue or a benign artifact.
Sample command: curl -sI https://example.com

requestresponse.request: GET / HTTP/1.1 Host: example.com User-Agent: HunterPy/2.0 (synthetic; for triage only) Accept: */*
requestresponse.response:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK Content-Type: text/plain
[HunterPy synthetic placeholder] This issue was imported from a HunterPy scan. Original module: ssl Original type: weak_key_size

issue.name: A record(s): 104.20.23.154, 172.66.147.243, 2606:4700:10::6814:179a, 2606:4700:10::ac42:93f3
issue.issueBackground: Classifier rationale: low severity score: 0.5
issue.remediationBackground: N/A — verify finding first.
issue.issueDetail:
Evidence: records: ['104.20.23.154', '172.66.147.243', '2606:4700:10::6814:179a', '2606:4700:10::ac42:93f3']
example.com resolves to 104.20.23.154, 172.66.147.243, 2606:4700:10::6814:179a, 2606:4700:10::ac42:93f3
Verification steps: 1. Reproduce the request that produced this finding. 2. Confirm the observation is consistent across retries. 3. Determine whether it is a real issue or a benign artifact.
Sample command: curl -sI example.com

requestresponse.request: GET example.com HTTP/1.1 Host: example User-Agent: HunterPy/2.0 (synthetic; for triage only) Accept: */*
requestresponse.response:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK Content-Type: text/plain
[HunterPy synthetic placeholder] This issue was imported from a HunterPy scan. Original module: dns_enum Original type: dns_a

issue.name: Missing security header: Content Security Policy
issue.issueBackground: Classifier rationale: known common type: missing_security_header
issue.remediationBackground: Add a strict Content-Security-Policy. Start with default-src 'self' and tighten from there.
issue.issueDetail:
The content-security-policy header is absent on this response.
Verification steps: 1. Re-fetch the page and grep the headers: curl -sI https://example.com | grep -i 'content-security-policy' 2. If the header truly never appears across any sample page, this is a real gap, not a one-off.
Sample command: curl -sI https://example.com
References: - https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/

requestresponse.request: GET / HTTP/1.1 Host: example.com User-Agent: HunterPy/2.0 (synthetic; for triage only) Accept: */*
requestresponse.response:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK Content-Type: text/plain
[HunterPy synthetic placeholder] This issue was imported from a HunterPy scan. Original module: headers Original type: missing_security_header

issue.name: Missing security header: Clickjacking Protection
issue.issueBackground: Classifier rationale: known common type: missing_security_header
issue.remediationBackground: Send `X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN` (or use CSP frame-ancestors).
issue.issueDetail:
The x-frame-options header is absent on this response.
Verification steps: 1. Re-fetch the page and grep the headers: curl -sI https://example.com | grep -i 'x-frame-options' 2. If the header truly never appears across any sample page, this is a real gap, not a one-off.
Sample command: curl -sI https://example.com
References: - https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/

requestresponse.request: GET / HTTP/1.1 Host: example.com User-Agent: HunterPy/2.0 (synthetic; for triage only) Accept: */*
requestresponse.response:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK Content-Type: text/plain
[HunterPy synthetic placeholder] This issue was imported from a HunterPy scan. Original module: headers Original type: missing_security_header

issue.name: Missing security header: MIME Sniffing Protection
issue.issueBackground: Classifier rationale: known common type: missing_security_header
issue.remediationBackground: Send `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` on every response.
issue.issueDetail:
The x-content-type-options header is absent on this response.
Verification steps: 1. Re-fetch the page and grep the headers: curl -sI https://example.com | grep -i 'x-content-type-options' 2. If the header truly never appears across any sample page, this is a real gap, not a one-off.
Sample command: curl -sI https://example.com
References: - https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/

requestresponse.request: GET / HTTP/1.1 Host: example.com User-Agent: HunterPy/2.0 (synthetic; for triage only) Accept: */*
requestresponse.response:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK Content-Type: text/plain
[HunterPy synthetic placeholder] This issue was imported from a HunterPy scan. Original module: headers Original type: missing_security_header

issue.name: Missing security header: HSTS
issue.issueBackground: Classifier rationale: known common type: missing_security_header
issue.remediationBackground: Send `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains` on HTTPS responses, then submit the domain to the HSTS preload list.
issue.issueDetail:
The strict-transport-security header is absent on this response.
Verification steps: 1. Re-fetch the page and grep the headers: curl -sI https://example.com | grep -i 'strict-transport-security' 2. If the header truly never appears across any sample page, this is a real gap, not a one-off.
Sample command: curl -sI https://example.com
References: - https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/

requestresponse.request: GET / HTTP/1.1 Host: example.com User-Agent: HunterPy/2.0 (synthetic; for triage only) Accept: */*
requestresponse.response:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK Content-Type: text/plain
[HunterPy synthetic placeholder] This issue was imported from a HunterPy scan. Original module: headers Original type: missing_security_header

issue.name: Missing security header: XSS Protection (legacy)
issue.issueBackground: Classifier rationale: known common type: missing_security_header
issue.remediationBackground: Set the x-xss-protection response header per OWASP guidance.
issue.issueDetail:
The x-xss-protection header is absent on this response.
Verification steps: 1. Re-fetch the page and grep the headers: curl -sI https://example.com | grep -i 'x-xss-protection' 2. If the header truly never appears across any sample page, this is a real gap, not a one-off.
Sample command: curl -sI https://example.com
References: - https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/

requestresponse.request: GET / HTTP/1.1 Host: example.com User-Agent: HunterPy/2.0 (synthetic; for triage only) Accept: */*
requestresponse.response:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK Content-Type: text/plain
[HunterPy synthetic placeholder] This issue was imported from a HunterPy scan. Original module: headers Original type: missing_security_header

issue.name: Missing security header: Referrer Policy
issue.issueBackground: Classifier rationale: known common type: missing_security_header
issue.remediationBackground: Send `Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin`.
issue.issueDetail:
The referrer-policy header is absent on this response.
Verification steps: 1. Re-fetch the page and grep the headers: curl -sI https://example.com | grep -i 'referrer-policy' 2. If the header truly never appears across any sample page, this is a real gap, not a one-off.
Sample command: curl -sI https://example.com
References: - https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/

requestresponse.request: GET / HTTP/1.1 Host: example.com User-Agent: HunterPy/2.0 (synthetic; for triage only) Accept: */*
requestresponse.response:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK Content-Type: text/plain
[HunterPy synthetic placeholder] This issue was imported from a HunterPy scan. Original module: headers Original type: missing_security_header

issue.name: Missing security header: Permissions Policy
issue.issueBackground: Classifier rationale: known common type: missing_security_header
issue.remediationBackground: Send a `Permissions-Policy` header explicitly disabling features your app doesn't need.
issue.issueDetail:
The permissions-policy header is absent on this response.
Verification steps: 1. Re-fetch the page and grep the headers: curl -sI https://example.com | grep -i 'permissions-policy' 2. If the header truly never appears across any sample page, this is a real gap, not a one-off.
Sample command: curl -sI https://example.com
References: - https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/

requestresponse.request: GET / HTTP/1.1 Host: example.com User-Agent: HunterPy/2.0 (synthetic; for triage only) Accept: */*
requestresponse.response:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK Content-Type: text/plain

issue.name: Dork suggestions: exposed_config (5 queries)
issue.issueBackground: Classifier rationale: module flagged as interesting
issue.remediationBackground: Remove sensitive files / endpoints surfaced by these dorks from public indexing; configure robots.txt and noindex headers; rotate any exposed credentials.
issue.issueDetail:
Evidence: template: exposed_config description: Configuration files exposing credentials queries: ['site:example.com filetype:env "DB_PASSWORD"', 'site:example.com filetype:env', 'site:example.com filetype:yaml "password"', 'site:example.com filetype:ini "database"', 'site:example.com filetype:config "connectionString"'] google_urls: ['https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+filetype%3Aenv+%22DB_PASSWORD%22', 'https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+filetype%3Aenv', 'https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+filetype%3Ayaml+%22password%22', 'https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+filetype%3Aini+%22database%22', 'https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+filetype%3Aconfig+%22connectionString%22'] bing_urls: ['https://www.bing.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+filetype%3Aenv+%22DB_PASSWORD%22', 'https://www.bing.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+filetype%3Aenv', 'https://www.bing.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+filetype%3Ayaml+%22password%22', 'https://www.bing.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+filetype%3Aini+%22database%22', 'https://www.bing.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+filetype%3Aconfig+%22connectionString%22'] ddg_urls: ['https://duckduckgo.com/?q=site%3Aexample.com+filetype%3Aenv+%22DB_PASSWORD%22', 'https://duckduckgo.com/?q=site%3Aexample.com+filetype%3Aenv', 'https://duckduckgo.com/?q=site%3Aexample.com+filetype%3Ayaml+%22password%22', 'https://duckduckgo.com/?q=site%3Aexample.com+filetype%3Aini+%22database%22', 'https://duckduckgo.com/?q=site%3Aexample.com+filetype%3Aconfig+%22connectionString%22'] mode: preview
Auto-generated dorks for this target. The tool did not query Google — you must review manually.
Verification steps: 1. These dorks were generated for template 'exposed_config'. They were NOT executed — Google scraping is off by default. 2. Open each URL below in your browser (or your AI of choice) to review the real Google results manually. • https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+filetype%3Aenv+%22DB_PASSWORD%22 • https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+filetype%3Aenv • https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+filetype%3Ayaml+%22password%22 • https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+filetype%3Aini+%22database%22 • https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+filetype%3Aconfig+%22connectionString%22 3. Sample command: # Sample query (paste into Google): site:example.com filetype:env "DB_PASSWORD"

issue.name: Dork suggestions: exposed_backups (3 queries)
issue.issueBackground: Classifier rationale: backup file found alongside admin path
issue.remediationBackground: Remove sensitive files / endpoints surfaced by these dorks from public indexing; configure robots.txt and noindex headers; rotate any exposed credentials.
issue.issueDetail:
Evidence: template: exposed_backups description: Backup files left in the web root queries: ['site:example.com ext:bak OR ext:old OR ext:backup', 'site:example.com "index of" backup', 'site:example.com inurl:backup filetype:sql'] google_urls: ['https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+ext%3Abak+OR+ext%3Aold+OR+ext%3Abackup', 'https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+%22index+of%22+backup', 'https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+inurl%3Abackup+filetype%3Asql'] bing_urls: ['https://www.bing.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+ext%3Abak+OR+ext%3Aold+OR+ext%3Abackup', 'https://www.bing.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+%22index+of%22+backup', 'https://www.bing.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+inurl%3Abackup+filetype%3Asql'] ddg_urls: ['https://duckduckgo.com/?q=site%3Aexample.com+ext%3Abak+OR+ext%3Aold+OR+ext%3Abackup', 'https://duckduckgo.com/?q=site%3Aexample.com+%22index+of%22+backup', 'https://duckduckgo.com/?q=site%3Aexample.com+inurl%3Abackup+filetype%3Asql'] mode: preview

issue.name: Dork suggestions: api_docs (3 queries)
issue.issueBackground: Classifier rationale: known common type: dork_suggestion
issue.remediationBackground: Remove sensitive files / endpoints surfaced by these dorks from public indexing; configure robots.txt and noindex headers; rotate any exposed credentials.
issue.issueDetail:
Evidence: template: api_docs description: Public API docs / Swagger UIs queries: ['site:example.com intitle:"api documentation"', 'site:example.com inurl:swagger OR inurl:swagger-ui', 'site:example.com inurl:"/api/v1" OR inurl:"/api/v2"'] google_urls: ['https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+intitle%3A%22api+documentation%22', 'https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+inurl%3Aswagger+OR+inurl%3Aswagger-ui', 'https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+inurl%3A%22%2Fapi%2Fv1%22+OR+inurl%3A%22%2Fapi%2Fv2%22'] bing_urls: ['https://www.bing.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+intitle%3A%22api+documentation%22', 'https://www.bing.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+inurl%3Aswagger+OR+inurl%3Aswagger-ui', 'https://www.bing.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+inurl%3A%22%2Fapi%2Fv1%22+OR+inurl%3A%22%2Fapi%2Fv2%22'] ddg_urls: ['https://duckduckgo.com/?q=site%3Aexample.com+intitle%3A%22api+documentation%22', 'https://duckduckgo.com/?q=site%3Aexample.com+inurl%3Aswagger+OR+inurl%3Aswagger-ui', 'https://duckduckgo.com/?q=site%3Aexample.com+inurl%3A%22%2Fapi%2Fv1%22+OR+inurl%3A%22%2Fapi%2Fv2%22'] mode: preview

issue.name: Dork suggestions: symfony_exposure (8 queries)
issue.issueBackground: Classifier rationale: module flagged as interesting
issue.remediationBackground: Remove sensitive files / endpoints surfaced by these dorks from public indexing; configure robots.txt and noindex headers; rotate any exposed credentials.
issue.issueDetail:
Evidence: template: symfony_exposure description: Symfony exposure patterns derived from SECREP1, SECREP2, and SECREP-symfony-upstream-issues reports. queries: ['site:example.com inurl:_profiler', 'site:example.com inurl:_wdt', 'site:example.com inurl:app_dev.php', 'site:example.com inurl:_profiler/phpinfo', 'site:example.com "Symfony Profiler"', 'site:example.com intext:"X-Debug-Token"', 'site:example.com inurl:_profiler/open file=', 'site:example.com ext:yml parameters database_password'] google_urls: ['https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+inurl%3A_profiler', 'https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+inurl%3A_wdt', 'https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+inurl%3Aapp_dev.php', 'https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+inurl%3A_profiler%2Fphpinfo', 'https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+%22Symfony+Profiler%22', 'https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+intext%3A%22X-Debug-Token%22', 'https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+inurl%3A_profiler%2Fopen+file%3D', 'https://www.google.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+ext%3Ayml+parameters+database_password'] bing_urls: ['https://www.bing.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+inurl%3A_profiler', 'https://www.bing.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+inurl%3A_wdt', 'https://www.bing.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+inurl%3Aapp_dev.php', 'https://www.bing.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+inurl%3A_profiler%2Fphpinfo', 'https://www.bing.com/search?q=site%3Aexample.com+%22Symfony+Profiler%22',
